text: The surgeon performs a multi-port cholecystectomy using three robotic arms. An atraumatic grasper, inserted through a five millimeter port, retracts the gallbladder over the liver.

The surgeon uses the Endoscope Plus camera in sensitive mode, providing enhanced visualization with a concentrated fluorescent green display of ICG permeated through the tissue, eliminating the need for additional lighting.

The surgeon uses Firefly imaging to enhance visualization of the cystic artery and gallbladder wall, dissecting the space above the artery to expose the critical view. Within the triangle of Coulomb, Firefly helps identify the cystic duct, common bile duct, and common hepatic duct, as well as the bifurcation of the left and right hepatic ducts. Clips are applied to the cystic artery and cystic duct, which are then transected using electrocautery.
The surgeon inspects the dissection bed before removing the gallbladder from the fossa, ensuring there are no visible aberrancies or ductal Luschka leaks.
